title: linaria genistifolia subsp. dalmatica
intro: Linaria genistifolia subsp. dalmatica, commonly known as Dalmatian toadflax, is a flowering plant species native to the Dinaric Alps region in Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The health benefits of Dalmatian toadflax include its potential to treat conditions such as hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and expectorant. In herbal preparations, Dalmatian toadflax is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its dried flowers and leaves can be used in traditional medicine.
